subject: Joint Distributions
question: The joint density function of $X$ and $Y$ is given by $f_{XY}(x,y) = \begin{cases} \frac{e^{-x/y}e^{-y}}{y} & x \ge 0, y \ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ Compute $\Pr(X > 1 | Y = y)$.
explanation: The marginal density function of $Y$ is $f_Y(y) = \frac{e^{-y}}{y} \int_{0}^{\infty} e^{-x/y}\,dx = \frac{e^{-y}}{y}\left[ -y e^{-x/y} \right]_0^{\infty} = e^{-y}$. Thus, $f_{X|Y}(x|y) = \frac{f_{XY}(x,y)}{f_Y(y)} = \frac{\frac{e^{-x/y}e^{-y}}{y}}{e^{-y}} = \frac{1}{y}e^{-x/y}$. Hence, $\Pr(X > 1 | Y = y) = \frac{1}{y}\int_{1}^{\infty} e^{-x/y}\,dx = -e^{-x/y}\big|_1^{\infty} = e^{-1/y}$. We end this section with the following theorem.
